title: rubus peracutiformis
intro: Rubus peracutiformis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Indian raspberry, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus peracutiformis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and roots are often used to make teas, decoctions, and ointments, which are applied topically or consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
